questions.q: In which field of cinema is Bimal Roy (1909-1966) best remembered in Hindi cinema?
questions.explanation: Bimal Roy was a celebrated film director known for bringing restrained social realism to Hindi cinema.

questions.q: Which 1953 Bimal Roy film about a poor farmer's struggle to save his land was honoured at the Cannes Film Festival?
questions.explanation: Do Bigha Zamin (1953) won the International Prize at the 1954 Cannes Film Festival.

questions.q: At which Kolkata-based studio did Bimal Roy receive his early training in filmmaking?
questions.explanation: Bimal Roy trained at New Theatres in Calcutta, where he began his career as a cameraman.

questions.q: Which 1955 Bimal Roy film, starring Dilip Kumar, was an adaptation of Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay's famous novel?
questions.explanation: Bimal Roy directed Devdas (1955), starring Dilip Kumar in the title role, based on Sarat Chandra's novel.

questions.q: Which 1958 Bimal Roy film, a reincarnation romance, starred Vyjayanthimala and Dilip Kumar?
questions.explanation: Madhumati (1958) was a reincarnation-themed romance directed by Bimal Roy starring Dilip Kumar and Vyjayanthimala.

questions.q: Which 1959 Bimal Roy film addressed the theme of caste discrimination and untouchability in Indian society?
questions.explanation: Sujata (1959) dealt with caste prejudice through the story of an untouchable girl raised in an upper-caste family.

questions.q: Bimal Roy was honoured as a master of which cinematic style in Hindi cinema?
questions.explanation: Bimal Roy is celebrated as a master of social realism for his restrained, humane portrayals of social issues.

questions.q: In which year was the acclaimed director Bimal Roy born?
questions.explanation: Bimal Roy was born in 1909 in Suapur, in what is now Bangladesh.

questions.q: Do Bigha Zamin, directed by Bimal Roy, centred on a farmer trying to save how much land from being seized?
questions.explanation: The title Do Bigha Zamin literally means 'two bighas of land', the small plot the farmer fights to keep.

questions.q: In which year did Bimal Roy pass away?
questions.explanation: Bimal Roy died in 1966 at the age of 56.

questions.q: Which actor played the rickshaw-puller Shambhu Mahato, the lead role in Bimal Roy's Do Bigha Zamin?
questions.explanation: Balraj Sahni gave a celebrated performance as the farmer-turned-rickshaw-puller Shambhu in Do Bigha Zamin.

questions.q: Which 1963 Bimal Roy film, set largely in a women's prison, marked Nutan's collaboration with the director?
questions.explanation: Bandini (1963), starring Nutan, was Bimal Roy's last completed film and a landmark of Hindi cinema.

questions.q: Which music director composed the songs for Bimal Roy's Madhumati (1958)?
questions.explanation: Salil Chowdhury composed the celebrated score of Madhumati, including songs like 'Aaja Re Pardesi'.

questions.q: Before becoming a director, in which technical role did Bimal Roy first establish himself at New Theatres?
questions.explanation: Bimal Roy worked as a cinematographer at New Theatres, notably shooting P.C. Barua's Devdas (1935).

questions.q: Which lyricist wrote the celebrated songs of Bimal Roy's Madhumati, including 'Suhana Safar'?
questions.explanation: Shailendra penned the lyrics for Madhumati, working alongside composer Salil Chowdhury.

questions.q: Which actress played the title role in Bimal Roy's Sujata (1959)?
questions.explanation: Nutan played the title character in Sujata, in one of her most acclaimed performances.

questions.q: Which 1953 Bimal Roy film, starring Ashok Kumar and Meena Kumari, was adapted from a Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay novella?
questions.explanation: Bimal Roy directed Parineeta (1953), based on Sarat Chandra's novella, starring Ashok Kumar and Meena Kumari.

questions.q: Which 1954 Bimal Roy film, based on a Sarat Chandra novel, won Kamini Kaushal acclaim for the title role?
questions.explanation: Biraj Bahu (1954), based on Sarat Chandra's novel, was directed by Bimal Roy and starred Kamini Kaushal.

questions.q: Do Bigha Zamin, directed by Bimal Roy, is widely regarded as a key film of which film movement?
options: Indian New Wave / Parallel cinema's precursor
questions.explanation: Do Bigha Zamin is seen as a pioneering work of Indian neorealism and a precursor to parallel cinema.

questions.q: Which actor played the convict Bikash, the love interest of Nutan's character, in Bimal Roy's Bandini (1963)?
questions.explanation: Ashok Kumar played Bikash in Bandini, opposite Nutan's Kalyani, with Dharmendra in another lead role.

questions.q: From which region of undivided India did Bimal Roy, a master of Bengali-rooted realism, originally hail?
questions.explanation: Bimal Roy was born in Bengal (in present-day Bangladesh) and brought a Bengali sensibility to Hindi cinema.

questions.q: Several future directors apprenticed under Bimal Roy. Which acclaimed director assisted him before his own career?
questions.explanation: Gulzar began his film career as an assistant and lyricist under Bimal Roy, writing 'Mora Gora Ang Laile' for Bandini.

questions.q: At the inaugural 1954 National Film Awards, which Bimal Roy film won the All India Certificate of Merit / top honour for Best Feature Film?
questions.explanation: Do Bigha Zamin won the President's Gold Medal for All India Best Feature Film at the first National Film Awards in 1954.

questions.q: Bimal Roy's Madhumati (1958) won the Filmfare Award for Best Film and set a record for most Filmfare wins that stood for decades. Roughly how many Filmfare Awards did it win?
questions.explanation: Madhumati won nine Filmfare Awards, a record that remained unbeaten until Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge in 1995.

questions.q: Which Bengali silent or early film by P.C. Barua did Bimal Roy famously work on as a cameraman in 1935?
questions.explanation: Bimal Roy was the cinematographer on P.C. Barua's 1935 Devdas at New Theatres before he later directed his own 1955 version.

questions.q: Which 1960 Bimal Roy film, with music by Salil Chowdhury, satirised an election and rural society in a Bengal village?
questions.explanation: Parakh (1960), directed by Bimal Roy with Salil Chowdhury's music, was a satire featuring the song 'O Sajna'.

questions.q: Bimal Roy won the Filmfare Award for Best Director a record number of times in his era. How many Best Director Filmfare Awards did he win?
questions.explanation: Bimal Roy won the Filmfare Award for Best Director seven times, including for Do Bigha Zamin, Madhumati, Sujata and Bandini.

questions.q: Bimal Roy's Do Bigha Zamin was partly inspired by a poem of the same theme by which Bengali literary figure?
questions.explanation: Do Bigha Zamin drew on Rabindranath Tagore's poem 'Dui Bigha Jomi' as a thematic inspiration.

questions.q: Which 1958 Bimal Roy film, based on Agha Hashr Kashmiri's play 'Yahudi Ki Ladki', starred Dilip Kumar and Meena Kumari and dealt with persecution of Jews in ancient Rome?
questions.explanation: Bimal Roy directed Yahudi (1958), starring Dilip Kumar and Meena Kumari, a costume drama based on Agha Hashr Kashmiri's Parsi-theatre play 'Yahudi Ki Ladki' about the persecution of Jews in Roman times.

questions.q: Which song from Bimal Roy's Bandini, picturised on a steamer, was famously the last song recorded by S.D. Burman as a singer for the film and sung in his own voice?
questions.explanation: 'O Jaanewale Ho Sake To Laut Ke Aana' from Bandini was sung by S.D. Burman himself in his distinctive voice.
